scheme_convergence.highlight: Jordan (FORS-based, variable Q) and Riva Labs NiceTry frameworks converging; both use ~2.4–2.6 KB signatures
Q=1 (rotate every tx) proposed as default; Q>1 useful for paymasters/bundlers needing parallel signing
Dilithium preferred for high-value/infrequent txs (hardware wallet + threshold); FORS/SPHINCS-minus for frequent/cheap txs

gas_and_parameters.highlight: FORS verify cost: ~35,000 gas; rotation (next address store): ~2,500 gas; params N=16, k=26, depth=5

formal_verification.highlight: Alex and Ricardo (ZKnox-adjacent) working on formal verification of signature contracts in Lean via Verity
ZKnox (Reno) interested in formally verifying Dilithium/MLDSA Solidity contract; funding is current blocker
Ricardo targeting Falcon SimpleWallet formal verification proof by end of month

upcoming_work.highlight: Call #10: Riva Labs (science laboratories) to demo MPC+Dilithium signing infrastructure
Blog post in progress (Antonio + Toma); draft to be shared in post-quantum transactions chat for feedback

action_items.action: Connect Alex/Ricardo with Riva Labs and Jordan teams on Verity-based formal verification
Engage LFG Labs (Verity team) for Yul/bytecode-to-Lean equivalence proofs
Share blog post draft in post-quantum transactions chat for community feedback

decisions.decision: Q=1 (per-tx key rotation) accepted as valid and likely default for both hardware and browser wallets
Canonical FORS params tentatively agreed: N=16, k=26, depth=5; ~2.4 KB signature size

targets.target: Call #10: Riva Labs demo of MPC + Dilithium signing
End of month: Falcon SimpleWallet formal verification proof (Ricardo)
